response.justification: Sarah has strong technical credentials and developer tools experience across CI/CD infrastructure, testing frameworks, and code generation. However, the resume lacks any demonstrated people management or team leadership experience, which is a required core competency for this role (2 years minimum). The job emphasizes full-time people-leadership with technical focus—she appears positioned as a strong IC rather than a manager. Worth a conversation to explore informal leadership or team-scaling experience not captured on the resume, but as written, a critical gap exists.
strengths: 16+ years software development experience across multiple platforms and languages, significantly exceeding minimum requirements
Demonstrated technical leadership through staff-level roles and substantial open-source contributions (GISCollective, Trial test runner, Fluent-Asserts testing library)
Hands-on developer tools expertise including CI/CD infrastructure (GitLab, Docker, Artifactory), testing frameworks, and code generation (MCP)
concerns: No explicit people management or team leadership experience mentioned—job requires 2 years minimum; teaching/mentoring alone doesn't demonstrate formal team development or performance management
Developer tools experience is part of broad full-stack background rather than specialized focus area; most roles emphasize technology breadth over productivity infrastructure depth
Limited modern mobile platform ecosystem knowledge; iOS/tvOS experience dates to 2015–2016 and predates current mobile development at scale

key_factors.factor: Strong technical leadership and developer tools background
Broad technical skills vs. specialized productivity focus
